overview: Boros Burn is a Standard Red Aggro variant that bypasses traditional board development to race opponents by dealing direct damage with spells and ETB effects. It aims to secure victory quickly before control or combo decks like Izzet Cauldron can establish their game plans. The deck leverages cheap creatures and burn spells to apply relentless pressure from turn one.
win_conditions: Dealing direct damage with burn spells and ETB effects to race the opponent
Using Slickshot Show-Off and Boros Charm for explosive lethal turns
Growing Hired Claw to apply sustained pressure over multiple turns

keep_criteria: Hands with one-drops like Fanatical Firebrand or Hired Claw
Hands with early burn spells like Plasma Bolt or Boltwave
Keep any hand with a land and a one-mana play (creature or burn).
Keep two lands if paired with a two-mana threat or efficient interaction like Boros Charm.
Prioritize hands that can execute a turn 1 creature or burn spell.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking one-mana creatures or early damage spells
Hands heavy on three-mana cards without early threats
Mulligan hands with no lands, three or more lands, or only expensive spells (>2 mana) with no early pressure.

examples.reason: Provides turn-one pressure and early damage potential.
Lacks early-game threats or burn to apply pressure before turn three.

early_game: Play one-drops like Fanatical Firebrand or Hired Claw to establish immediate pressure
Use cheap burn spells like Plasma Bolt to clear blockers or push damage
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a one-mana creature or burn spell.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast a two-mana threat or interaction.
If opening with two lands, play both, then cast your best two-mana play.
Always prioritize establishing board presence over hoarding mana
the deck wins by dealing damage, not surviving.
mid_game: Leverage Slickshot Show-Off's Plot ability to play it for free, followed by Boros Charm for explosive damage
Use Screaming Nemesis to block life gain and punish blockers
Treat mana as a resource to convert into damage, not a pool to hoard.
Keep one mana open for interaction if facing control, but commit aggressively against non-interactive decks.
Manage hand size by prioritizing high-impact spells
discard redundant burn if you have lethal in hand.
Graveyard is generally irrelevant for Boros Burn, so don't worry about it unless facing specific graveyard strategies.
Commit all threats when the opponent has no removal or counterspells.
late_game: Rely on Hired Claw's growth or finish with accumulated burn
Use sideboard cards like Rest in Peace or Magebane Lizard to shut down specific strategies if the game drags on
Direct damage spells and unblocked creatures.
The goal is to reach 10-12 damage in a single turn or over two turns.
Slickshot Show-Off can provide immediate burst damage or finish the game on entry.
Go all-in when you have lethal damage, when the opponent has no interaction, or when you're ahead in the race and they have no way to stabilize.
If you have a lethal combination in hand, play it immediately.

key_cards.name: Fanatical Firebrand
key_cards.notes: 1/1 Haste enables Plasma Bolt's Void for extra damage and removes X/1 creatures.

key_cards.name: Hired Claw
key_cards.notes: Best red one-drop; grows each turn to maintain steam.

key_cards.name: Viashino Pyromancer
key_cards.notes: Deals 2 ETB damage and acts as a lizard for Hired Claw.

key_cards.name: Slickshot Show-Off
key_cards.notes: Plot ability allows free casting, synergizes lethally with Boros Charm.

key_cards.name: Screaming Nemesis
key_cards.notes: Punishes blockers, blocks life gain, and has a decent body.

key_cards.name: Boros Charm
key_cards.notes: Broad damage spell, protects against sweepers, and enables lethal combat with Double Strike.

key_cards.name: Boltwave
key_cards.notes: Unconditional three-mana damage spell for one mana.

key_cards.name: Plasma Bolt
key_cards.notes: Triggers Void with Fanatical Firebrand or opponent's removal for three damage.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize playing Fanatical Firebrand before casting Plasma Bolt to trigger its Void ability for extra damage.
Set up Slickshot Show-Off's Plot ability early, then play it for free followed by Boros Charm or other spells to maximize damage.
Consider focusing on growing Hired Claw over traditional burn lines in certain matchups to maintain pressure across multiple turns.
Use Screaming Nemesis to block life gain against specific archetypes when it's the difference between victory and defeat.
Common mistake: hoarding mana or holding back threats out of fear of removal.
Boros Burn wins by dealing damage, not surviving.
Prioritize the 2 damage to opponent or 1 damage to creature over drawing a card unless you're desperate for cards.
Hexing Squelcher is a versatile tool
use it to shut down key permanents or as a 2/2 body.

matchups.advice: Side in Rest in Peace to lock graveyards and force specific answers. Use Magebane Lizard to counter Prowess strategies.
Side in Fire Magic to clear X/1 creatures and tokens.
Side in Sunspine Lynx to punish greedy mana bases.
Side in Magebane Lizard to disrupt spell-heavy turns.
Side in Magebane Lizard to counter multi-spell turns.

Cast threats in response to land drops or when you can't be countered. Use Boros Charm to draw cards or deal damage around counters. If facing heavy counters, consider more uncounterable threats or discard to remove the spell in response.

Outpace them. Play as fast as possible. Use direct damage to interrupt their ramp spells or kill their early threats. Hexing Squelcher is excellent here to shut down mana rocks or enchantments before they can snowball.

Stabilize with efficient removal and life gain if available. Use Boros Charm's life gain to survive the early turns, then pivot to closing the game with your own threats. Don't overcommit to trading; focus on surviving until you can stabilize.

Pressure relentlessly. Force them to use resources on your threats. Use Boros Charm to draw cards and maintain hand size. If they try to stabilize, have burn ready to close the game. Don't let them get comfortable; keep the pressure on.

It's a race. Focus on card advantage and efficient interaction. Use Boros Charm to draw cards and deal damage. Prioritize killing their threats before they can block or trade. If you're ahead, go all-in; if behind, look for ways to disrupt their game plan and stabilize.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (hold up mana or cast threats in response to land drops), targeted removal (save creatures for when they're tapped or use charm to kill), and sweepers (have burn ready to close the game before or after the wipe).
Pay attention to opponent's life total and card advantage engines.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Red Aggro variant that forgoes permanent board power in favor of dealing damage to the opponent with spells and ETB effects

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: forgoes permanent board power in favor of dealing damage to the opponent with spells and ETB effects
grows by spending mana to increase its power each turn... keep our steam for more turns

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: enabling Plasma Bolt 's Void to turn it into another three-damage spell
set up to play it for free, followed by other noncreature spells to remove blockers or pressure the clock
focusing on it before playing other cards is better than blindly going for the traditional Burn line
ability to block life gain can be the difference between victory and defeat

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: troublesome X/1 creatures, like Deep-Cavern Bat
protection against sweepers like Split Up and Day of Judgment
larger creatures like Beza, the Bounding Spring or troublesome blockers
matters against the Enduring Curiosity cycle or Unstoppable Slasher
